events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a fillet weld that is undersized versus the callout, with a gauge shown in a photo. Since I don't have the actual photo, I need to estimate based on the description. The condition is "fillet undersized vs. callout" which means it's out of tolerance. AWS D1.1 allows some tolerance for fillet weld sizes - typically undersize up to 1/16" is allowed for fillet welds up to 1/4", but if it's clearly undersized beyond tolerance, it's a fail.

Given the scenario says "fillet undersized vs. callout (gauge in photo - ME)" and asks to estimate whether it's within tolerance, and the task says "state why it is out of tolerance," this is clearly a fail condition.

Let me construct the JSON response.

  "findings": [
    "Fillet weld leg size is undersized relative to the WPS/callout dimension as confirmed by welding gauge measurement",
    "Undersized fillet reduces effective throat and load-carrying capacity of the structural connection"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Mark and document the deficient weld location for rework",
    "Build up the fillet weld to the specified leg size per AWS D1.1 repair procedures",
    "Re-inspect with fillet weld gauge after rework and record acceptance"
  ],
  "rationale": "The fillet weld gauge reading shows the weld leg is below the called-out size. AWS D1.1 permits only limited undersize tolerance (typically up to 1/16 in. for fillets up to 1/4 in., and proportionally limited for larger sizes), but a measured undersize beyond that tolerance is a rejectable discontinuity. Because the connection is structural and the weld is rough-complete but not yet closed out, it must be corrected before acceptance.",
  "references": [
    "AWS D1.1 - Fillet weld size and tolerance requirements",
    "AWS D1.1 - Inspection and acceptance criteria for welded structural joints"
  ]
}
